title: 万圣节前夜的谋杀
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\126-万圣节前夜的谋杀-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
After discussing the acceptance of murder with Miss Emlyn, Poirot is introduced to Elizabeth Whitaker, who is suggested as a person of interest regarding the death of Joyce Reynolds. Poirot explains he is investigating privately through a friend rather than the police.

Whitaker, a guest at the party, describes the evening's events. She recounts that during the final program—a "dragon hunt" game involving burning grapes—she stepped into the hallway to escape the heat. There, she observed Mrs. Dreckly (referred to elsewhere as Mrs. Dribb) emerging from a first-floor washroom carrying a large, heavy vase of flowers and autumn leaves. As Mrs. Dreckly stood at the staircase landing, she appeared to be staring intently toward the library door, which is located directly opposite the dining room door. Whitaker notes that Mrs. Dreckly seemed suddenly startled, as if she had seen something unexpected, such as a door opening or a person appearing. This shock caused Mrs. Dreckly to lose her grip, shattering the vase and spilling water on the hall floor.

Whitaker believes Mrs. Dreckly was frightened by something she saw near the library, though Mrs. Dreckly did not mention being scared at the time. Poirot observes that Mrs. Dreckly is not easily frightened and suggests that someone may have been in the library and retreated inside upon hearing the vase break, only to slip away once the guests were leaving. Whitaker concludes by detailing the party's sequence of games, which included a broom race, a balloon race, a mirror trick involving reflections, an obstacle race, a flour and sixpence game, dancing, and dinner.
